tax: Placed at genus rank, Slagling is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: an obsidian-glass body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a brittle glass body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a jagged black-glass figure. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — black glass flakes and cut-marks — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Slagling occupies quenched obsidian margins. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a fast quench freezes lava to glass and recur with a fresh quench re-forms it, sustaining themselves on residual heat.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: grinds forward to slash, bursts when struck. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: glass body and razor edges. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: cutting and shrapnel-burst. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Slagling matters because cuts crews and scatters shrapnel. It lets survey reports sort brittle glass forms from molten ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Slagling is the genus-rank grouping of obsidian-glass elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a razor-edged obsidian body that bursts into shrapnel when struck, and divide below into species groups by glass quench and residual heat. A walking blade of volcanic glass.
